title: Prometheus Storage Full / TSDB Compaction Issues
symptoms: Alert: PrometheusLocalStorageCompactionFailed or PrometheusNotIngestingSamples
Prometheus UI shows 'Error on ingesting samples that are too old or out of order'
kubectl -n monitoring describe pod prometheus-* shows OOMKilled
Metrics gaps in Grafana dashboards
causes: Persistent volume for Prometheus is full
Retention period too long for the available storage
High cardinality metrics causing excessive TSDB growth
TSDB compaction falling behind (Prometheus overloaded)
investigation: kubectl -n monitoring get pvc | grep prometheus
# Check TSDB stats via API: curl http://localhost:9090/api/v1/label/__name__/values | jq length
remediation: Expand PVC: kubectl -n monitoring patch pvc <pvc-name> -p '{"spec":{"resources":{"requests":{"storage":"50Gi"}}}}'
Reduce retention: edit PrometheusRule CR or Prometheus spec: spec.retention: 7d
Find high-cardinality metrics: Prometheus UI > Status > TSDB Status
Delete old blocks manually: exec into pod, rm -rf /prometheus/chunks_head/*
If OOMKilled: increase memory limit in Prometheus spec: spec.resources.limits.memory
Restart Prometheus (non-destructive): kubectl -n monitoring rollout restart statefulset/prometheus-kube-prometheus-stack-prometheus
escalation: Prometheus storage full causes monitoring blindness. All alerts stop working. Escalate immediately — treat as P1 for observability.

title: Prometheus Scrape Failures / Target Down
symptoms: Alert: PrometheusTargetMissing firing
Prometheus UI > Targets shows targets in DOWN state
Missing metrics for specific services in Grafana
Alert: KubeServiceDown or specific service alerts silent
causes: ServiceMonitor label selector not matching Service labels
Service or pod not running (unrelated to Prometheus)
Network policy blocking Prometheus scrape port
TLS certificate mismatch on scrape endpoint
Metrics endpoint path changed in new service version
investigation: kubectl -n monitoring logs prometheus-kube-prometheus-stack-prometheus-0 | grep -i 'scrape error' | tail -50
# Prometheus UI: Status > Targets — check exact error message
kubectl get svc -n <target-namespace> -o yaml | grep -i label
kubectl get networkpolicy -A  # check if scrape port is blocked
remediation: Fix ServiceMonitor selector: kubectl -n monitoring edit servicemonitor <name>
Verify service labels match selector: kubectl get svc <name> -n <ns> --show-labels
Test scrape manually: kubectl -n monitoring exec -it prometheus-* -- curl http://<service>:<port>/metrics
For network policy: add egress rule allowing Prometheus to reach the target port
For TLS issues: update ServiceMonitor spec.endpoints.tlsConfig
escalation: Missing scrape targets for critical services should be escalated to the owning team to fix their ServiceMonitor configuration.

title: Alertmanager Not Sending Notifications
symptoms: Alerts firing in Prometheus but no notifications received (no Slack/email/webhook)
Alertmanager UI shows alerts but Status is 'Active' without any receivers
Alert: AlertmanagerFailedToSendAlerts
SRE Agent not receiving Alertmanager webhook events
causes: Alertmanager configuration error (wrong webhook URL, expired credentials)
Network connectivity to receiver endpoint from cluster
Alert silenced in Alertmanager
Alert inhibited by another alert
Alertmanager pod not running
investigation: # Alertmanager UI: Status > Config — verify config is valid
# Test webhook: kubectl -n monitoring exec <alertmanager-pod> -- curl -X POST http://sre-agent.sre-agent.svc.cluster.local:8080/incidents/webhook/alertmanager
remediation: Fix Alertmanager config: edit the secret alertmanager-kube-prometheus-stack-alertmanager
Reload config: kubectl -n monitoring exec alertmanager-* -- kill -HUP 1
Remove bad silences from Alertmanager UI
Test webhook manually to verify SRE Agent is reachable
Restart Alertmanager: kubectl -n monitoring rollout restart statefulset/alertmanager-*
escalation: Silent alerting is a monitoring failure. All incidents may go unnoticed. Treat as P1 for incident management.

title: Grafana Dashboard Not Loading / Data Source Error
symptoms: Grafana shows 'Error: 502 Bad Gateway' or 'No data'
Dashboard panels show 'datasource not found'
Grafana pod in CrashLoopBackOff
Users cannot access Grafana UI
causes: Grafana pod restarted and lost session (non-persistent SQLite DB)
Prometheus data source URL changed
Grafana PVC full (if persistent storage configured)
Grafana version incompatible with dashboard JSON
Memory limit too low causing OOMKill
investigation: kubectl -n monitoring get pvc | grep grafana
kubectl -n monitoring get svc | grep grafana
remediation: Restart Grafana: kubectl -n monitoring rollout restart deploy/kube-prometheus-stack-grafana
Verify data source: Grafana UI > Configuration > Data Sources > Test
Fix Prometheus URL in data source if changed
If PVC full: expand it or purge old dashboard versions
Increase memory: kubectl -n monitoring edit deploy/kube-prometheus-stack-grafana, add resources.limits.memory
escalation: Grafana outage prevents dashboard access. Alert delivery via Alertmanager is unaffected. Notify on-call team of visibility gap.

title: kube-state-metrics or node-exporter Not Running
symptoms: Alert: KubeStateMetricsDown or NodeExporterDown
Missing Kubernetes object metrics (pod count, deployment replicas, etc.)
Missing node metrics (CPU, memory, disk, network)
Grafana 'Kubernetes / Nodes' dashboard shows no data
causes: DaemonSet or Deployment pod evicted or crashed
Resource limits too tight (OOMKill)
Node exporter port conflict
RBAC permission revoked for kube-state-metrics
remediation: Restart kube-state-metrics: kubectl -n monitoring rollout restart deploy/kube-prometheus-stack-kube-state-metrics
Restart node-exporter: kubectl -n monitoring rollout restart ds/kube-prometheus-stack-prometheus-node-exporter
For OOMKill: increase memory limit in helm values and apply
For RBAC: verify ClusterRoleBinding for kube-state-metrics service account
escalation: Loss of kube-state-metrics or node-exporter causes alert blindness for cluster health. Escalate if pods cannot restart after 2 attempts.
